title: Olive Oil on Linen
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes; powder + Dawn, AIR-DRY FLAT, structured = pro
stainChemistry: Olive oil on linen follows the same protocol as cotton (powder + Dawn) but with two linen-specific complications: (1) structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen warps under wet handling — pro from minute one, and (2) linen wrinkles aggressively when wet, so AIR-DRY FLAT is non-negotiable (hanging stretches the fiber). Recurring traps stay the same: water-first instead of powder-first (spreads oil), and hot dryer with residual oil (locks it as permanent yellow shadow).
whyThisWorks: Powder preload pulls bulk oil up before water spreads it. Dawn emulsifies the olive oil. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY FLAT locks nothing. Linen's natural wrinkle behavior means the flat-dry step matters more than on cotton.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): olive oil is mostly oleic acid (monounsaturated triglyceride). Linen is cellulose, similar to cotton — tolerates Dawn + cool wash. Pro uses POG / petroleum ether for oil + NSD on structured linen.
defaultAssumption: Cooking spatter or salad dressing drip on a simple flat washable linen napkin / shirt / tablecloth. Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro. Simple flat washable linen: cover oil spot with cornstarch / baking soda. 20–30 min absorb. Soft-brush off.
Generous Dawn directly. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, cool water, drying rack
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when hung wet. Inspect dry. NEVER hot dryer with residual oil.

steps: 1. Care label triage — structured / vintage = pro.
2. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min, soft-brush off.
3. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
4. Cool wash + AIR-DRY flat. No heat with residual oil.
warnings: Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one
Powder BEFORE water — water-first spreads oil
NEVER hot dryer with residual oil — yellow shadow
Linen air-dries FLAT — hanging stretches the fiber

homeSolutions.agent: Powder First, Dawn Second, AIR-DRY Flat
homeSolutions.instruction: Olive oil on linen follows the same powder + Dawn sequence as cotton, with two linen-specific notes: structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen goes to pro from minute one, and linen wrinkles aggressively when wet so AIR-DRY FLAT is non-negotiable. Two hard rules: powder BEFORE water (water-first spreads oil into a wider ring), and NO hot dryer with residual oil (locks it as a permanent yellow shadow).
Find the care tag. Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro. Simple flat washable linen (napkin, plain shirt, basic tablecloth) = home candidate.

homeSolutions.agent: Cornstarch or Baking Soda — 20 to 30 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the oil spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 20 to 30 minutes. Soft-brush off.
Generous Dawn dish soap directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 minutes. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when wet, hanging stretches the fiber. Inspect dry. NEVER hot dryer with residual oil — heat polymerizes the oil into the linen as a permanent yellow shadow.

steps: 1. Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one.
2. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min, soft-brush off.
3. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
5. Repeat Dawn pass if yellow shadow remains.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen (pro). Stop if hot dryer was used on residual oil (often permanent yellow shadow). Stop after 3 home cycles plateau.
warnings: Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one
Powder BEFORE water — water-first spreads oil
NEVER hot dryer with residual oil

neverDo: Never water-first on oil — spreads it
Never hot dryer with residual oil — yellow shadow
Never iron over a yellow shadow — sets it
fiberSensitivities: Simple flat washable linen: tolerates Dawn + cool wash
Structured / lined linen: pro — wet handling warps
Linen-rayon blend: pro — rayon doesn't tolerate aggressive Dawn

materialWarnings: Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one
Powder before water — water-first spreads oil
Air-dry FLAT — linen wrinkles and stretches when hung wet
No hot dryer with residual oil — yellow shadow

escalation.when: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen. Hot dryer used on residual oil. 3 home cycles plateau. Aged buildup over weeks/months.
escalation.whatToTell: "Olive oil on [simple / structured / lined / vintage] linen, age [fresh / hours / weeks of buildup], home-treated with [cornstarch preload + Dawn cool + cool wash + air-dry flat]. No hot dryer. [Residual yellow shadow]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with linen experience

contextQuestions: Cooking spatter, salad dressing, or aged buildup?
Simple flat linen, structured / lined, or vintage?
Fresh, hours old, or weeks of hot-dried buildup?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Linen-cotton blends follow the same protocol. Linen-rayon blends go to pro because rayon doesn't tolerate the Dawn dwell. AIR-DRY FLAT is non-negotiable on any linen — hanging stretches the fiber.
